titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward Roe to Under Secretary, 22 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward Roe

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Edward Roe to Sir Robert Chalmers requesting the names featured on the list for next year's High Sheriff Office.
Colonel Edward Roe (1841-1920), a retired Army Medical Officer, was serving as the high sheriff for Cavan in 1916. The High Sheriff of Cavan was the British Crown's judicial representative in County Cavan, Ireland from the 16th century until 1922, when the office was abolished in the new Free State and replaced by the office of Cavan County Sheriff.
Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) was appointed under-secretary of Ireland in 1916, replacing Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the aftermath of the Easter Rising.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Sheriff's Office
Cavan, 22nd June 1916 Shrievalty Sir I shall be obliged if you
will let me know the names of
the two gentlemen who were on
last this year's list along with mine. I want to know these names so
that I can furnish list for the coming year. I am,
Sir,
Your obdt. Servant
E.A.H. Roe,
High Sherriff The Under Secretary
The Castle, Dublin Inform Col Roe that the names of the
undermentioned gentlemen were
returned in addition to his own at Summer Assizes 1915 as
eligible to serve the Office of the High
Sherriff during 1916, viz:—
Joseph Arthur Benison, Esq.,
Slieve Russell, Ballyconnell.
Theophilus Edward Lucas-Clements, Esq.,
Rathkenny, Cootehill.
ChoS.
26-6-16 Done 27/6 desp'd
27/6
